description: This dual-axis plot presents the room-temperature Charpy V-notch impact energy and Brinell hardness as functions of phosphorus content for age-hardened Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels. The left vertical axis and black circular markers denote impact energy in J/cm², while the right vertical axis and black square markers represent Brinell Hardness Number (HBW). The horizontal axis shows phosphorus content ranging from 0 to 0.05 wt.%. The steels were aged at 530°C for 10 h, corresponding to the Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C base composition with approximately 1 wt.% Si. The data reveal a strong inverse correlation: as phosphorus increases from near 0 to approximately 0.045 wt.%, impact energy drops sharply from about 138 J/cm² to roughly 25 J/cm², while hardness concurrently rises from about 290 to 340 HBW. This figure demonstrates that phosphorus is highly detrimental to notch toughness, reducing it by over 80%, even though it marginally increases aged hardness. The caption states this behavior reflects phosphorus segregation during solidification and promotion of κ-carbide precipitation along austenite grain boundaries.
